action.reasoning:
Observation: After 3 retraining attempts, MultiRoom-N4-S5, LockedRoom, Dynamic-Obstacles still fail (~0% SR). Even with FullyObsWrapper. Time: 3.74h left, 22 iters. My best eval was iter4 with SR=0.10, but I've not re-evaluated since. Current checkpoints have ~6-7 envs solved → expected SR ~0.6-0.7. Critical: I need to submit eval NOW to lock in this score before more experiments.

Analysis: Time risk is high - I've been training without evaluating. Let me submit eval immediately to confirm the current state, then plan the final ~3h of experiments. The FullyObs experiments may have hurt some envs if predict.py needs updating - but if I haven't updated predict.py, then the FullyObs-trained checkpoints will fail to load properly OR load and then evaluate with ImgObs wrapper (mismatched obs space). Let me check predict.py first.

Actually iter6 promote_best.py copied .best.zip → .zip for the 3 hard envs. Then iter7 (train_hard2.py) overwrote them with new checkpoints. Then iter8 (train_full.py) trained with FullyObsWrapper to checkpoints_full/. So the main 'checkpoints/' dir likely has iter7 results. But did iter8 train_full.py overwrite checkpoints/ too? Let me check workspace_files: there are checkpoints_full/ separately. Good - iter8 saved to checkpoints_full only.

Decision: Submit eval NOW with current checkpoints/ (which has all 10 envs trained, with the 7 working envs and 3 failing-but-still-trained hard envs). predict.py uses ImgObsWrapper for all - matches the checkpoints/ dir.
